findings.title: VirtualToken.cashIn mints by msg.value for ERC20 underlyings, enabling unbacked minting/mis-accounting

best_agent_match.title: VirtualToken cashIn for ERC20 underlyings uses msg.value for minting amount

findings.title: Permissionless createLaunchPad can consume per-block vETH loan quota and DoS other launches

best_agent_match.title: LamboFactory createLaunchPad has no verification of pool creation success

findings.title: Router sell pricing uses full vETH reserves including debt-locked liquidity, causing sell reverts and exit lockups

best_agent_match.title: VirtualToken _update allows transferring tokens without considering incoming transfers for debt position

findings.title: buyQuote refund logic withholds 1 wei from overpayments

best_agent_match.title: VirtualToken cashIn for ERC20 underlyings uses msg.value for minting amount

findings.title: Rebalance initialization can be seized if deployment is non-atomic or proxy is left uninitialized

best_agent_match.title: Public token initializer allows first-caller ownership of any uninitialized instance supply

findings.title: Launchpad creation reverts because factory transfers LP tokens to the zero address

best_agent_match.title: Launchpad creation can revert permanently when burning LP tokens to zero address

rejected_candidates.title: Virtual debt is created but has no practical settlement path in launch flow
rejected_candidates.reason: Mostly overlaps with F-003’s debt-locked-liquidity root cause/impact; presented path is design-level and not a clearly separate exploit primitive.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance executes permissionless flash-loan trades with no enforced output bound
rejected_candidates.reason: Swaps are atomic with the flash-loan transaction and `rebalance` enforces post-trade profitability (`profit > 0`) or reverts, so no demonstrated protocol fund-loss path from public callers.

rejected_candidates.title: Public token initializer allows first-caller ownership of any uninitialized instance supply
rejected_candidates.reason: Factory deployment path initializes clones atomically in the same transaction; no practical front-run window in normal protocol flow.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded infrastructure addresses without chain guard create unsafe deployment assumptions
rejected_candidates.reason: Operational/deployment-configuration risk rather than an in-protocol exploitable vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Router _sellQuote executes swap before fee calculation, enabling front-running fee extraction
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: if later checks fail, the entire transaction reverts, including the swap; no partial execution value loss is left on-chain.

rejected_candidates.title: VirtualToken _update allows transferring tokens without considering incoming transfers for debt position
rejected_candidates.reason: Debt-floor enforcement on sender is intentional; receiving tokens without auto-repay is by design and does not bypass debt-transfer restrictions.

rejected_candidates.title: VirtualToken cashIn for ERC20 underlyings uses msg.value for minting amount
Router lacks deadline parameter allowing stale execution
rejected_candidates.reason: Best-practice gap, but `minReturn` already provides execution-price protection; no distinct exploit demonstrated.

rejected_candidates.title: LamboFactory createLaunchPad has no verification of pool creation success
rejected_candidates.reason: For Uniswap V2 factory semantics, `createPair` reverts on failure and returns a deployed pair on success; `address(0)` success path is not realistic.

rejected_candidates.title: VirtualToken has no pause mechanism for emergency response
rejected_candidates.reason: Absence of a pause feature alone is not a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Router getBuyQuote and getSellQuote don't account for fee in amountOut calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: `getSellQuote` and `_sellQuote` both compute AMM output then deduct fee from output consistently.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance directionMask accepts any uint256 allowing pool address manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: Arbitrary masks can make caller-supplied rebalance attempts revert, but do not create a protocol-level exploit or permissionless fund-loss path.

rejected_candidates.title: Router fee rate can be set to 100% causing total user funds loss
rejected_candidates.reason: Privileged-owner parameterization/trust-model concern, not a permissionless vulnerability.
